questions.text:
Computers represent different types of data in binary.

Tick (✓) one box to show the reason why computers use binary to represent data.

Option | Tick
A Computers only allow 1s and 0s to be entered.
B Computers are made of switches and gates that can only be on or off.
C Binary does not need to be converted into other forms of data to be displayed.
D Both computers and humans can quickly process binary data.

rows: A Computers only allow 1s and 0s to be entered.
B Computers are made of switches and gates that can only be on or off.
C Binary does not need to be converted into other forms of data to be displayed.
D Both computers and humans can quickly process binary data.

questions.text:
Computers represent different types of data in binary. One form of data is characters. The American standard code for information interchange (ASCII) denary number for the character 'N' is 78.

Tick (✓) one box to identify the ASCII denary number for the character 'Q'.

Option | Tick
A 81
B 80
C 79
D 77

Computers represent different types of data in binary. One form of data is characters. The American standard code for information interchange (ASCII) denary number for the character 'N' is 78.

Give the binary number for the ASCII denary number for 'N'.

Working space

Computers represent different types of data in binary. One form of data is characters.

Explain how the word 'RED' is represented using a character set.

marking_points.text: Unique binary/denary number given/stored for each character
The code for R is stored, then the code for E then D in sequence

questions.text:
Computers represent different types of data in binary. Sound can be represented as binary.

Explain why recording sound with a higher sampling resolution creates a more accurate recording.

marking_points.text: More bits allocated to each amplitude
A wider range of amplitudes can be recorded

questions.text:
Computers represent different types of data in binary. Sound can be represented as binary.

Give one other way that the accuracy of a sound recording can be improved.

A computer has a central processing unit (CPU). The CPU includes the registers: Program counter (PC), Memory address register (MAR), Memory data register (MDR), Accumulator (ACC), Current instruction register (CIR).

The table contains five statements about the role of registers in the fetch–decode–execute cycle of a CPU.

Letter | Statement
A | PC stores the address of the next instruction to be accessed
B | MAR stores the instructions of the program that is running
C | MDR stores the data passed to it from the PC
D | ACC stores the result of each calculation
E | CIR stores the instruction currently being executed

Two of the statements are not correct.

Identify the letter of each incorrect statement. Suggest a corrected statement for each.

Incorrect statement 1
Corrected statement

Incorrect statement 2
Corrected statement

visuals.description: Table of five statements about registers in the fetch-decode-execute cycle
rows: PC stores the address of the next instruction to be accessed
MAR stores the instructions of the program that is running
MDR stores the data passed to it from the PC
ACC stores the result of each calculation
CIR stores the instruction currently being executed

answers.scoring_rule: One mark for letter and One mark for matching correction.

marking_points.text: …Data is from bus not PC // Data is from address in MAR not PC

questions.text:
A computer has a central processing unit (CPU). The computer has a single core 3.5 GHz processor and an 8 kB cache.

State what is meant by a 3.5 GHz processor.

marking_points.text: It can run 3.5 billion FE cycles each second // it can execute 3.5 billion instructions each second

questions.text:
A computer has a central processing unit (CPU). The CPU is changed to a dual-core 3.5 GHz processor.

Explain how the number of cores affects the performance of a CPU.

marking_points.text: More cores mean more FDE cycles/instructions are executed each second
…because each core runs an FE cycle/instruction simultaneously

questions.text:
A computer has a central processing unit (CPU). The amount of cache is increased to 64 kB.

Explain how the amount of cache affects the performance of a computer.

marking_points.text: …because more cache means the processor can access more frequently used data/instructions faster
…instead of having to access the data from the slower-access RAM

questions.text:
A computer has both primary and secondary storage. Random access memory (RAM) is one example of primary storage.

Describe what is meant by RAM.

marking_points.text: Stores data for the processor to access directly/quickly // directly accessed by the CPU

questions.text:
A computer has both primary and secondary storage. Read only memory (ROM) is another example of primary storage. ROM often stores the start-up instructions for a computer.

Identify one other item of data that is commonly stored in ROM.

A computer has both primary and secondary storage. Some computers make use of virtual memory.

State the purpose of virtual memory.

marking_points.text: To run programs when there is insufficient RAM to run them
To allow RAM to store more data when required

questions.text:
A computer has an operating system (OS).

Complete the table by writing each missing OS function name and description.

Function name | Description
managing memory | (blank)
(blank) | allows application software to run on the computer
managing peripherals | (blank)

visuals.description: OS functions table with missing names and descriptions
rows: allows application software to run on the computer

rows.definition: allows application software to run on the computer

marking_points.text: managing memory: allocates memory to processes // prevents two processes accessing the same memory
managing peripherals: allocates data to buffers // transmits data to hardware // receives data from hardware

questions.text:
A computer has an operating system (OS). The OS handles interrupts.

State the purpose of an interrupt.

marking_points.text: To indicate that something requires the attention of the processor/OS/CPU

questions.text:
A computer has an operating system (OS). The OS handles interrupts.

Identify one input device and one example of an interrupt that it can generate.

Input device
Interrupt

answers.scoring_rule: One mark for input device and matching interrupt:

questions.text:
A computer has an operating system (OS). The OS handles interrupts. A processor is currently executing a program. The processor receives an interrupt.

Explain how the processor manages the current program and the interrupt.

marking_points.text: Processor finishes current FE cycle for program
Processor checks interrupt priority queue // processor checks for higher priority interrupt than program/process
If lower priority processor runs next FDE cycle for program/process // if lower priority processor continues with program/process
(if higher priority) processor stores current process/registers on stack
If there is another higher priority interrupt (than process) then repeat
… (otherwise) processor retrieves content of stack/registers/previous process (to continue with process from program)

questions.text:
A student enters a uniform resource locator (URL) into their web browser.

Draw and annotate a diagram to show the process from the user entering the URL to the web browser displaying the web page.
questions.topic_name: The Internet and the World Wide Web

answers.scoring_rule: Any five marks for each part of diagram:

marking_points.text: DNS storing table/database of URL and IPs // DNS finding IP for URL
DNS sending IP to computer / browser
DNS sending to higher DNS if not found
Web browser/computer sending request to IP of web server
Web page data sent from server to computer / web browser

questions.text:
Complete the description of digital currencies using the terms provided.

blockchains  changed  datachains  decentralised  deleted  encryption  passport  physical address  physically  records  time-stamp  traced  transaction-chains  virtually

A digital currency does not exist [blank], it can only be accessed electronically.

Some digital currencies have digital ledgers called [blank]

These are decentralised databases where each transaction is stored as a new set of data with a [blank] and is linked to the previous set of data. This means that transactions cannot be altered, only new transactions added, which allows the location of the data to be [blank]

answers.scoring_rule: One mark for each term in correct place:

questions.text:
A self-driving tractor sows seeds and harvests crops for a farmer. The self-driving tractor moves automatically through the field, turning at each end and stopping when it has finished.

One reason the self-driving tractor is a robot is because it has a mechanical structure that includes wheels, an engine, and a framework.

Give one other reason why the self-driving tractor is an example of a robot.

marking_points.text: It has electrical components // by example

questions.text:
A self-driving tractor sows seeds and harvests crops for a farmer. If a person is detected within 3 metres whilst the tractor is moving, the self-driving tractor must stop.

Explain how an infra-red sensor, microprocessor and actuator can be used to stop the self-driving tractor if a person is detected within 3 metres.

marking_points.text: Sensor continuously sends the digitised value / reading / distance to the microprocessor
Microprocessor compares the data / signal to the stored data of a person and distance of 3m
If the data / signal is less than (or equal to) a person and within 3m
…a signal is sent to actuator to make the tractor stop / apply the brakes
If the data/signal is greater than 3m no action is taken
If stopped and data/signal is not a person and/or more than 3m a signal is sent to actuator to make the tractor start
The whole process repeats until turned off

questions.text:
A self-driving tractor sows seeds and harvests crops for a farmer.

Identify one other sensor that the self-driving tractor might use and how it will use the sensor.

Sensor
Use

answers.scoring_rule: One mark for sensor and One mark for matching use:

marking_points.text: Accelerometer … …to adjust for uneven ground // to detect if the tractor crashes
Proximity … …to detect if near the end of the field // to detect other obstacles
Light … … to identify when to turn the headlights on

questions.text:
A self-driving tractor sows seeds and harvests crops for a farmer.

Explain the drawbacks of a farmer using a self-driving tractor to sow seeds and harvest crops.

marking_points.text: Farmer may need reskilling in how to use it …
… and not recognise a person and fails to stop

questions.text:
A self-driving tractor sows seeds and harvests crops for a farmer. The self-driving tractor has an in-built expert system that the farmer can use to solve any errors with the tractor.

Identify three components of an expert system.

1
2
3

A self-driving tractor sows seeds and harvests crops for a farmer. At the end of each day the self-driving tractor transmits the data it has collected to the farmer's computer in their house. The transmission uses an echo check.

Describe the role of the self-driving tractor and the farmer's computer in the echo check.

marking_points.text: The same data is transmitted back (from computer to tractor)
Tractor compares both sets of data
… if they are identical there is no error // reverse
Tractor sends confirmation of accuracy if the same // Tractor resends the data if they are different // Tractor transmits error if they are different

questions.text:
A self-driving tractor sows seeds and harvests crops for a farmer. At the end of each day the self-driving tractor transmits data to the farmer's computer. Another method of error checking is a parity block check.

The table shows 7 bytes that are transmitted using odd parity. The parity bit has been completed for each byte.

Complete the parity byte for the data.

(blank) | parity bit | bit 7 | bit 6 | bit 5 | bit 4 | bit 3 | bit 2 | bit 1
byte 1 | 1 | 1 | 0 | 0 | 1 | 1 | 1 | 0
byte 2 | 1 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 1 | 1 | 0
byte 3 | 0 | 1 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0
byte 4 | 0 | 1 | 0 | 0 | 1 | 1 | 1 | 1
byte 5 | 1 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0
byte 6 | 0 | 1 | 1 | 1 | 1 | 1 | 1 | 1
byte 7 | 1 | 1 | 0 | 0 | 1 | 1 | 0 | 1
parity byte | (blank) | (blank) | (blank) | (blank) | (blank) | (blank) | (blank) | (blank)

visuals.description: Parity block check table with 7 bytes using odd parity; parity byte row is blank

answers.scoring_rule:
One mark for two correct
Two marks for five correct
Three marks for all eight correct

questions.text:
Data is transmitted using the internet. The data is encrypted before transmission.

State the purpose of encrypting data.

marking_points.text: If the data is intercepted, it cannot be understood

questions.text:
Data is transmitted using the internet. The data is encrypted before transmission.

Describe the differences between symmetric and asymmetric encryption.

marking_points.text: Both the sender and receiver know the key
Asymmetric has different keys // a public key and a private key
…public to encrypt the data and private to decrypt
…anyone can know the public key but only those intended know the private key

questions.text:
Data is transmitted using the internet. The data is transmitted using packet switching.

Identify two items of data contained in a packet header.

1
2
questions.topic_name: Types and Methods of Data Transmission

questions.text:
Data is transmitted using the internet. The data is transmitted using packet switching.

Give the purpose of a router in the packet-switching process.
questions.topic_name: Types and Methods of Data Transmission

marking_points.text: Control the route the packet takes
Send each packet towards its destination

questions.text:
Hexadecimal can be used to represent binary numbers.

Convert the denary number 236 into hexadecimal.

Working space

Hexadecimal can be used to represent binary numbers.

Give one benefit of using hexadecimal instead of binary to represent data.

marking_points.text: Less likely for humans to make mistakes
Easier for humans to spot errors/debug

questions.text:
Two's complement can be used to represent positive and negative binary integers.

Convert the two's complement integer 10110111 into denary. Show all your working.

answers.scoring_rule: One mark for working, one mark for answer

questions.text:
Two's complement can be used to represent positive and negative binary integers.

Convert the denary number 45 into an 8-bit two's complement integer.

Working space

Logical shifts are performed on binary integers.

State the mathematical effect of a right shift of four places on a positive binary integer.
